DLT = Two switches

1) Triggered vs Continuous
   → How often does it run?

   Triggered   → Runs once | Continuous → Runs continuously


2) Development vs Production
   → What happens to compute when stopped?

   Development → Compute persists | Production  → Compute terminates

Databricks SQL Warehouse performance issues:

• Slow single query → increase cluster SIZE (vertical)
• Slow during high concurrency → increase MAX CLUSTERS (horizontal)
• Queries waiting → check Peak Queued Queries

Cost when idle  → Auto Stop ✅
Too many users → Max clusters
Slow queries   → Cluster size
Convenience    → Serverless

The question wants the basics — sum, max, count — grouped by Store. Option B does exactly that, nothing fancy.
D is the "distracted" option — it looks right (groups by Store) but gets distracted and wanders off into avg and countDistinct, which nobody asked for.
So when you see an aggregation question: check the group-by column first, then verify every aggregation matches exactly what's asked. D fails the second check.

%sql
-- Working equivalent of what the exam CLAIMS Option D does:
-- Step 1: COLLECT_LIST(items) → [[1,100,200,300], [1,250,300]]
-- Step 2: AGGREGATE folds ARRAY_UNION across the collected arrays
-- Step 3: Result: [1, 100, 200, 300, 250]
SELECT cartId, 
  AGGREGATE(
    COLLECT_LIST(items), 
    CAST(ARRAY() AS ARRAY<INT>), 
    (acc, x) -> ARRAY_UNION(acc, x)
  ) AS items
FROM carts
GROUP BY cartId
